description_long: This object consists of two distinct red mailboxes: one is a large, rectangular public mailbox with a flat top and a mail slot on the front, while the other is a smaller, cylindrical rural-style mailbox mounted on a metal pole with a curved top and a visible red flag. The public mailbox features a blue label above the mail slot and stands on four short legs, giving it a sturdy, urban appearance. The rural mailbox has a front-hinged door with a white label and a flag for outgoing mail, and is supported by a single angled metal post. Both mailboxes are painted in a bright, slightly weathered red finish, with some visible surface texture and wear. The public mailbox is designed for high-volume mail collection, while the rural mailbox is intended for individual or residential use. The overall construction appears to be primarily metal, with some plastic or painted elements for signage and the flag.
description: A pair of red mailboxes: one large, rectangular public mailbox with a front mail slot and blue label, and one smaller, cylindrical rural mailbox on a metal pole with a red flag.
description_view_0: Front view showing both the large public mailbox with its mail slot and blue label, and the smaller rural mailbox with its flag and label.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical shape of the rural mailbox and the depth of the public mailbox.
description_view_2: Front view of both mailboxes, clearly showing the mail slot and labels on each.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the public mailbox and the side of the rural mailbox, with the flag visible.
